annotations.label: Uniform convergence (informal)

anchor.snippet: converges uniformly to a limiting function

annotations.note: `TendstoUniformly F f p` captures the lead's informal notion of a sequence/filter of functions converging uniformly to a limit.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: is uniformly convergent on

annotations.note: Uniform convergence on a set is `TendstoUniformlyOn F f p s`, the on-set version of the article's ε–N definition.

annotations.label: Cauchy criterion for uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: the Cauchy criterion can be used to give an equivalent alternative formulation for uniform convergence

annotations.note: The equivalence is captured by `TendstoUniformlyOn.uniformCauchySeqOn` together with `UniformCauchySeqOn.tendstoUniformlyOn_of_tendsto`.

annotations.label: Uniform (supremum) metric characterization

anchor.snippet: we can characterize uniform convergence of

annotations.note: `Metric.tendstoUniformlyOn_iff` restates uniform convergence using the metric (sup) characterization on a metric target.

annotations.label: Locally uniformly convergent

anchor.snippet: is said to be locally uniformly convergent with limit

annotations.note: Local uniform convergence is `TendstoLocallyUniformly` (with the on-set variant `TendstoLocallyUniformlyOn`).

annotations.label: Uniform convergence into a metric space

anchor.snippet: One may straightforwardly extend the concept to functions

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `TendstoUniformly` in a `UniformSpace` codomain, which subsumes the metric-space generalization stated in the article.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence of nets into a uniform space

anchor.snippet: The most general setting is the uniform convergence of nets of functions

annotations.note: `TendstoUniformly F f p` is filter-indexed (nets via their tail filter) into a `UniformSpace`, the most general form described.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence (hyperreal)

anchor.snippet: Uniform convergence admits a simplified definition in a hyperreal setting

annotations.note: No nonstandard/hyperreal characterization of uniform convergence was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basic example: x^n vs x^n on [0,1]

anchor.snippet: a basic example of uniform convergence can be illustrated as follows

annotations.note: No worked example of the sequence x^n on [0,1] as a uniform-convergence illustration is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pointwise but not uniformly convergent sequence

anchor.snippet: is a classic example of a sequence of functions that converges to a function

annotations.note: No formalized counterexample sequence demonstrating pointwise but not uniform convergence was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence of exponential series

anchor.snippet: The series expansion of the exponential function can be shown to be uniformly convergent

annotations.note: No direct lemma states the exp power series converges uniformly on bounded sets; only auxiliary results like `TendstoUniformlyOn.comp_cexp` exist (the Weierstrass M-test gives it as an invocation).

annotations.label: Weierstrass M-test

anchor.snippet: Theorem (Weierstrass M-test)

annotations.note: `tendstoUniformlyOn_tsum` is the Weierstrass M-test: a summable majorant `u` with ‖f n x‖ ≤ u n forces uniform convergence of the partial sums.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence implies local uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: Every uniformly convergent sequence is locally uniformly convergent.

annotations.note: `TendstoUniformly.tendstoLocallyUniformly` is exactly the implication uniform ⇒ locally uniform.

annotations.label: Local uniform convergence implies compact convergence

anchor.snippet: Every locally uniformly convergent sequence is compactly convergent

annotations.note: The forward direction of `tendstoLocallyUniformlyOn_iff_tendstoUniformlyOn_of_compact` (and `tendstoLocallyUniformly_iff_forall_isCompact` on locally compact spaces) gives uniform convergence on each compact set.

annotations.label: Local uniform and compact convergence agree on locally compact spaces

anchor.snippet: For locally compact spaces local uniform convergence and compact convergence coincide.

annotations.note: `tendstoLocallyUniformly_iff_forall_isCompact` proves the equivalence under `LocallyCompactSpace`.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence iff uniformly Cauchy (complete image)

anchor.snippet: is uniformly convergent if and only if it is uniformly Cauchy

annotations.note: Together with `TendstoUniformlyOn.uniformCauchySeqOn`, this gives the uniform-Cauchy ⇔ uniformly-convergent equivalence.

annotations.label: Dini's theorem

anchor.snippet: is a monotone increasing sequence

annotations.note: `Monotone.tendstoUniformlyOn_of_forall_tendsto` is Dini's theorem: monotone pointwise convergence to a continuous limit on a compact set is uniform.

annotations.label: Equicontinuous pointwise-convergent sequence converges uniformly

anchor.snippet: Uniform convergence is also guaranteed if

annotations.note: `Equicontinuous.tendsto_uniformFun_iff_pi` (and `EquicontinuousOn.tendsto_uniformOnFun_iff_pi`) gives uniform = pointwise convergence for equicontinuous families on a compact (resp. compact-covered) space.

annotations.label: Uniform limit theorem

anchor.snippet: Uniform limit theorem — Suppose

annotations.note: `TendstoUniformlyOn.continuousOn` (and `TendstoUniformly.continuous`) prove that a uniform limit of continuous functions is continuous.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence and differentiation

anchor.snippet: is a sequence of differentiable functions on

annotations.note: `hasFDerivAt_of_tendstoUniformly` (and `hasDerivAt_of_tendstoUniformly`) give the article's differentiation-under-uniform-limit theorem.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence and Riemann integration

anchor.snippet: is a sequence of Riemann integrable functions defined on a compact interval

annotations.note: `TendstoUniformlyOn.tendsto_intervalIntegral_of_continuousOn` shows uniform convergence on `[a,b]` lets one swap limit and integral.

annotations.label: Uniform limit of analytic functions is analytic

anchor.snippet: Using Morera's Theorem

annotations.note: `TendstoLocallyUniformlyOn.differentiableOn` formalizes the result that a locally uniform limit of holomorphic (= complex-analytic) functions on an open set is holomorphic.

annotations.label: Pointwise, uniform, and absolute convergence of series

anchor.snippet: converges: pointwise on E if and only if the sequence of partial sums

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Summable`/`HasSum` for pointwise and `HasSumUniformlyOn` for uniform series convergence, but bundles them via filters rather than the article's three-way pointwise/uniform/absolute definition.

annotations.label: Uniformly convergent series of continuous functions

anchor.snippet: be contained in the set E and each f n be continuous

annotations.note: Continuity of the sum follows by composing `tendstoUniformlyOn_tsum` with `TendstoUniformlyOn.continuousOn`; no single dedicated lemma was found.

annotations.label: Term-by-term integration of uniformly convergent series

anchor.snippet: each f n is integrable on E

annotations.note: Mathlib has uniform-limit interchange with the integral but no single named term-by-term-integration-of-a-series result; it must be assembled via `tendstoUniformlyOn_tsum` plus the integral swap.

annotations.label: C[0,1] with supremum norm is a Banach space

anchor.snippet: denote the space of real-valued or complex-valued continuous functions on the unit interval

annotations.note: Specializing `BoundedContinuousFunction.instCompleteSpace` to `α = [0,1]`, `β = ℝ` or `ℂ` gives that C[0,1] with the sup norm is a Banach space.

annotations.label: Completeness of C[0,1]

anchor.snippet: follows from the completeness of the real or complex numbers

annotations.note: Completeness of C[0,1] specializes `BoundedContinuousFunction.instCompleteSpace`, which itself requires only completeness of the codomain.

annotations.label: C(X) is a Banach space for compact Hausdorff X

anchor.snippet: is a compact Hausdorff space

annotations.note: For compact X, continuous functions are automatically bounded, so `BoundedContinuousFunction.instCompleteSpace` gives the Banach-space structure on C(X).

annotations.label: Bounded functions with sup norm form a Banach space

anchor.snippet: of all bounded real-valued or complex-valued functions on

annotations.note: `UniformOnFun.instCompleteSpace` (and `BoundedContinuousFunction.instCompleteSpace` for the continuous bounded sub-Banach space) covers uniform completeness, but Mathlib does not single out the space of *all* bounded functions with the sup norm as a named Banach space.

annotations.label: L^infinity and its relation to uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: essentially bounded measurable functions on

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.Lp f ⊤ μ` is the L^∞ space of essentially bounded measurable functions; the link to uniform convergence (norm = essential sup) is implicit in the L^∞ norm definition.

annotations.label: Almost uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: converges almost uniformly on E if

annotations.note: Mathlib has no named definition of `almost uniform convergence`; only Egorov-style statements that produce the conclusion are present.

annotations.label: Egorov's theorem

anchor.snippet: Egorov's theorem does guarantee that on a finite measure space

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.tendstoUniformlyOn_of_ae_tendsto` is Egorov's theorem on finite measure sets.

annotations.label: Almost uniform convergence implies a.e. convergence and convergence in measure

anchor.snippet: Almost uniform convergence implies almost everywhere convergence

annotations.note: Without a named `almost uniform convergence`, the two implications are not packaged; Mathlib does have a.e. ⇒ in-measure (`MeasureTheory.tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_ae_of_measurable_edist`) which gives the harder half once the definition is unfolded.

annotations.label: Uniform metric on bounded functions

anchor.snippet: we can equip the space of bounded real or complex

annotations.note: `BoundedContinuousFunction` carries the sup-norm/uniform metric, and `UniformFun`/`UniformOnFun` provide the metric on general bounded function spaces.

annotations.label: Compact-open topology on C(Y) for σ-compact locally compact Hausdorff Y

anchor.snippet: this topology is generated by the seminorms

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.compactOpen` defines the compact-open topology on `C(X, Y)`; under σ-compact locally compact Hausdorff X this is the Fréchet seminorm topology, though the σ-compact-Fréchet packaging itself is not a separate named decl.
